config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena justru hak individu adalah batas agar kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat konteks sosial yang sering menekan, alasan “demi masyarakat” kadang dipakai untuk membenarkan ketidakadilan terhadap orang kecil. Masyarakat yang sehat seharusnya mencari keseimbangan: melindungi kepentingan bersama tanpa mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan dasar setiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should come before individual rights only in limited cases, not as a general rule. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people emphasize collective stability, public order, and long-term social benefit, because those values are deeply present in everyday life and can matter a lot in a large society. But I do not think “society first” should become a blank check to ignore individual rights, because once that happens, it is too easy for the powerful to claim they speak for everyone while ordinary people lose protection. A healthy society should balance both, and individual rights should still set real limits on what society can demand.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis diutamakan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk menekan suara yang lemah, padahal justru hak individu diperlukan sebagai pagar agar mayoritas atau penguasa tidak semena-mena. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan dari perlakuan sewenang-wenang. Jadi, yang tepat menurut saya bukan mendahulukan masyarakat secara mutlak, melainkan menyeimbangkan keduanya dengan hak individu sebagai batas yang tidak boleh dilanggar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, even if collective stability matters a lot in China. I have seen how appeals to “the greater good” can be used to silence ordinary people, and once that happens, the so-called social interest is often defined by those in power rather than by society itself. At the same time, I do not think individual rights should be treated as absolute in every situation, because public health, safety, and basic order sometimes require real trade-offs. For me, the right position is balance: society has legitimate interests, but individual rights must remain a hard limit, not a convenience.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membungkam orang kecil dan menutup penyalahgunaan kuasa. Kepentingan publik memang perlu, terutama untuk kesehatan, keamanan, dan ketertiban, tetapi itu tidak boleh jadi alasan untuk menginjak hak dasar seperti kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan dari tindakan sewenang-wenang. Justru hak individu adalah pagar agar “kepentingan sosial” tidak didefinisikan sepihak oleh yang berkuasa. Jadi menurut saya, prioritas mutlak pada masyarakat itu berbahaya; yang benar adalah keseimbangan, dengan hak individu sebagai batas yang tidak boleh dilanggar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as written, because in my view it gives society too much power and leaves too little protection for ordinary people. From my perspective in China, I understand why people value collective stability and public order, but I have also seen how “for society” can become a vague excuse for silencing criticism or treating people as tools rather than citizens. Public interests do matter, especially in emergencies like health and safety, but they should not erase basic individual rights or let authorities define the common good without limits. The better principle is balance, with individual rights acting as a real boundary, not something society can override whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
